title: How to Apply
summary: Resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, and outreach steps for AEM developer roles in India.

sections.title: Apply workflow
sections.summary: Prepare your resume and profiles first. When applying, check that the role's expected joining timeline is reasonably compatible with your availability.
steps: Draft your resume with an AI assistant if useful: Share your real experience — companies, technologies, education, projects, and employment history. State the AEM role you are targeting and the type of companies you are interested in. List your actual skills clearly and ask for a role-focused draft using relevant ATS keywords. Review every bullet manually and remove anything you cannot explain or defend in an interview.

Do not publish an AI-generated resume draft as-is. If you use Overleaf, start with a simple one-column template and move the reviewed content into it carefully. Check formatting, dates, links, section order, and every bullet before exporting the PDF. Do not add GitHub, HackerRank, or other profile links unless you actively use them.

Upload the Overleaf PDF to an AI assistant and a few free ATS checker sites. Use ATS checker scores as a rough sanity check, not as a hiring benchmark. Review missing keywords, parsing issues, section structure, and role alignment before deciding what to change.

Review LinkedIn and Naukri section by section so your role, dates, skills, notice period, and experience are consistent with your resume. An AI assistant can help review individual sections, but verify every suggestion before publishing it. Use clear role and skill terminology that matches the work you actually do and the roles you are targeting. Spend real time on both profiles — they are essential for India hiring. Use the same resume details on other job portals. Keep LWD (last working day), location, CTC, notice period, and availability accurate.

Notice period — prioritise roles that match your availability: If you are serving a long notice period and a posting explicitly asks for an immediate or very early joiner, prioritise other roles unless the employer indicates flexibility. Applying to roles with a clearly incompatible joining timeline can lead to an early rejection and may not be the best use of your application effort.

Notice period — where you can apply: During a long notice period, you can still apply where the posting or recruiter indicates flexibility on joining dates. Some large employers run longer hiring processes or accept candidates serving standard notice periods, but this varies by role and team. State your last working day clearly and check the expected joining timeline.

As your last working day gets closer, increase application and outreach activity for roles seeking earlier joiners. Around the final month of notice can be a useful point to widen daily searches, but always follow the joining expectations in the posting or recruiter conversation. Use early notice time to prepare your resume, LinkedIn, Naukri, company shortlist, and AEM interview prep.
sections.body: Prioritise roles whose joining timeline matches your availability. If a posting explicitly requires an immediate joiner and you cannot start soon, focus your effort on better-matched openings.

sections.title: Outreach
sections.summary: Use referrals, HR posts, and connection requests — avoid blind apply when you can reach a person.
steps: LinkedIn posts: Search AEM (or your role keyword), filter type to Posts. First pass — wider time window; after that — last 1 day only. Look for HR or hiring posts for your role. Often an email is in the post — send your application there. This gives you a direct application channel when a recruiter or hiring contact has explicitly shared their contact details.

Gmail cover-letter template: For LinkedIn or Naukri invites, keep a reusable Gmail template — short cover letter with skills, experience, LWD if applicable, contact details, and resume attached. Reuse for every invite-style apply. For roles seeking early joiners, time this outreach closer to your availability. If the employer accepts a longer notice period, you can contact them earlier and state your last working day clearly.

Referral before direct apply: When you see a job on LinkedIn or Naukri, do not apply immediately. Search that company on LinkedIn, find people in the same role (or a related AEM role), and send a connection request. After they accept, ask for a referral politely — not bluntly ("could you refer me?"). Use an AI-drafted message that mentions your role, experience, and why you fit. Wait for their reply. If the person is comfortable referring you, follow their process. If you do not receive a response, continue with the official application route rather than waiting indefinitely.
Recruiter direct: If the post lists a recruiter email or contact info, reaching out there can be another route alongside a portal apply.

Staffing / HR agencies: If a staffing agency is the stated hiring channel for a role, follow that route and verify the recruiter and employer details before sharing personal documents. You can also check whether the same opening appears on the employer's official careers site.
sections.body: Use targeted outreach alongside official applications when you can identify a relevant recruiter, hiring contact, or potential referrer. Keep templates in Gmail; refine referral and HR-post wording with an AI assistant once, then reuse.

sections.title: Train your LinkedIn feed
sections.summary: Shape LinkedIn so AEM jobs and hiring posts show up near the top when you open the app.
steps: Join AEM-focused LinkedIn groups (see Community links on this page) and turn on notifications for the most active ones.
Follow AEM recruiters, hiring managers, and delivery leads who regularly post openings. Connect with people already in AEM roles at your target companies.
Follow and engage with relevant AEM recruiters, engineers, groups, and hiring posts so your LinkedIn activity stays focused on the roles and topics you care about.

Search AEM (Posts + Jobs) daily with a last-1-day filter once your notice window is open. Save useful searches and repeat them regularly instead of relying only on the home feed to surface relevant openings.
sections.body: A focused network and repeatable search routine can reduce the time spent finding relevant AEM hiring posts.

sections.title: Careers sites vs job boards
sections.summary: AEM openings can appear across employer careers sites, LinkedIn, Naukri, recruiter posts, and other job boards. The same opening may not be equally visible on every channel.
steps: Do not rely only on the Careers button. Recruiters often message you on Naukri or LinkedIn, or post on Foundit, Cutshort, and similar boards.
In Companies, open each employer's detail panel and check AEM evidence and Hiring evidence links — those often point to board posts or searches that prove recent AEM hiring.
Keep Naukri and LinkedIn profiles complete and current so inbound outreach can find you when the careers site is quiet.
Use job boards and recruiter outreach as additional discovery channels while treating the employer's official application route as the primary source for role details.
sections.body: Careers pages are useful, not complete. Evidence and hiring-evidence links in this playbook help you find where roles actually appear.

sections.title: Learning sources
sections.summary: Keep job-search prep and AEM technical prep separate.
steps: Job-search content: An AI assistant can help draft resume bullets, profile copy, and outreach messages. Provide only your real experience and remove any claim you cannot verify or explain.
AEM technical depth: This playbook's roadmaps, glossary, and interview prep — plus Adobe Experience League when you need to verify a topic before an interview.
Employer research: Use the Companies table and company evidence before you interview or message someone at that employer.
Do not list tools or platforms on your resume or profiles unless you use them actively.
sections.body: AI tools can help structure and review your profile. This playbook focuses on AEM career research, learning paths, and interview preparation.

sections.title: Weekly rhythm
sections.summary: How to search and apply on LinkedIn and Naukri after your profiles are ready.
steps: Before the last 30 days of notice: Focus on resume, profiles, company shortlists, and AEM interview prep. You may apply to MNCs and product companies that accept 60–90 day notice if your LWD matches — but avoid immediate-joiner roles if you cannot start that soon.

Last 30 days of notice: On LinkedIn and Naukri, search your AEM role, set the time filter to last 30 days for a first pass, then switch to last 1 day for daily new listings. Apply only where the role fits your skills and your LWD — skip immediate-joiner posts if you could not actually start that soon.
Ongoing daily search: After the backlog is cleared, search the same role with last 1 day only — apply daily to new listings while you are within your availability window.

LinkedIn posts: Search AEM (or your keyword), type Posts, wider window first then last 1 day on repeat visits. Check HR hiring posts and email applications (see Outreach) when your availability matches the role's joining expectations.

Platform hygiene: Use the same search strategy on LinkedIn and Naukri. Review your Naukri profile regularly and keep availability, notice period, preferred locations, skills, and experience current. Update the profile when these details change or when you improve the way your experience is presented.
Stay patient: Response rates can vary throughout a notice period. If early responses are slow, continue preparing and keep your availability clear as your joining date gets closer.
sections.body: Prepare early and adjust application intensity as your availability changes. Roles seeking earlier joiners may become more relevant as your last working day approaches.

sections.title: Tools
sections.summary: Common tools for resume, profiles, and applications in India.
steps: ChatGPT or another AI assistant — useful for drafting, restructuring, and reviewing resume content, LinkedIn/Naukri sections, and outreach messages. Verify every output against your real experience.
Overleaf — one-column LaTeX resume template; manual paste from the draft, export PDF.
ATS checker tools — useful for spotting parsing, structure, and keyword gaps. Scores vary by tool, so use them as diagnostics rather than a hiring benchmark.
LinkedIn — profile, job search (30-day then 1-day filter), Posts search for HR hiring, company people search for referrals.
Naukri — keep your profile aligned with your resume and LinkedIn; keep availability, notice period, preferred locations, and skills current.
Gmail — saved template for cover letter and resume when applying via invite or email from a post.
This playbook — Companies, filters, and interview prep once conversations start.
sections.body: Avoid automated mass-application workflows. Use a reviewed base resume and consistent profile information, then adjust role-specific keywords or emphasis only when the changes accurately reflect your experience.

sections.title: Community
sections.summary: Independent LinkedIn groups for AEM developers — discovery only; not run by this playbook.
steps: Open the Community section (sidebar → Community) for LinkedIn group links. These are independent peer groups — not administered here and not affiliated with Adobe.
Join only if the group's rules fit you. Share openings and ask questions politely — do not spam resumes into discussions.
Combine group leads with the Companies list and your own LinkedIn/Naukri search.
sections.body: Community links live in the Community chapter so they stay in one place (also linked from How to Apply).
